dice.name: Diceritory
dice.tagline: Two dice, endless tactics: every roll starts a new battle for every inch of the board.

tetromino.name: Tetritory
tetromino.tagline: Your favorite Tetris pieces enter the battle for territory — stack, surround, and win.

domino.name: Dominotory
domino.tagline: The numbers have to match — your ambitions don't: turn every domino into a foothold for conquest.

hex.name: Hexoritory
hex.tagline: A hexagonal board, six directions of attack — master the hexes and surround your rivals.

route.name: Routeritory
route.tagline: Roll the die and carve a path across mountains and a river — bridges keep moving, so cross while you can.

autoWin.label: Auto-win
autoWin.hint: End the game early once the outcome can no longer change

doublesExtraTurn.label: Doubles grant an extra turn
doublesExtraTurn.hint: If both dice match, the same player rolls again

autoFillEnclosed.label: Auto-claim enclosed territory
autoFillEnclosed.hintTetromino: If a player encloses an area with their pieces, it's immediately filled with tetrominoes in their color
autoFillEnclosed.hintHex: If a player encloses an area with hexes, it's immediately filled with shapes in their color
autoFillEnclosed.hintRoute: If a player fully encloses an area with their own territory (river and mountains don't count as a border — only the player's own cells do), it's immediately claimed for them

dice.rotationHint: Turn off for a harder game — the block can only be placed exactly as rolled
dice.smartHint: Toward the end of the game, the dice are more likely to roll a move you can actually make

tetromino.rotationHint: Turn off for a harder game — the piece can only be placed in the orientation it was drawn in
tetromino.smartHint: Toward the end of the game, you're more likely to draw a piece you can actually place

domino.rotationHint: Turn off for a harder game — the domino can only be placed exactly as rolled, its halves can't be swapped
domino.smartHint: Toward the end of the game, you're more likely to roll a domino you can actually place

hex.rotationHint: Turn off for a harder game — the shape can only be placed in the orientation it was drawn in
hex.smartHint: Toward the end of the game, you're more likely to draw a shape you can actually place

placingLabel.dice: Hover over the board and click to place the rectangle.
placingLabel.tetromino: Hover over the board and click to place the piece.
placingLabel.domino: Hover over the board and click to place the domino.
placingLabel.hexStored: Hover over the board and click to place the shape from storage.
placingLabel.hexRoll: Hover over the board and click to place the shape ({{count}} remaining).
placingLabel.route: Hover over the board and click to claim a cell.

playing.skippedLabel: No valid positions for this roll. Turn skipped.

log.diceRollSkipped: {{name}}: dice {{d1}}×{{d2}} — no valid moves, turn skipped{{doubleNote}}
log.tetrominoRollSkipped: {{name}}: piece {{type}} — no valid moves, turn skipped
log.dominoRollSkipped: {{name}}: domino {{a}}:{{b}} — no valid moves, turn skipped
log.dominoRollFromStorageSkipped: {{name}}: domino from storage {{a}}:{{b}} — no valid moves, turn skipped
log.routeRollSkipped: {{name}}: die {{roll}} — no valid moves, turn skipped
log.routeRevived: {{name}}: a bridge moved and opened up a move again — back in the game

gameOver.reasonDecided: Game ended early — the opponent(s) could no longer catch up to the leader
gameOver.reasonMajority: Game ended early — a player claimed more than an equal share of the board

common.cornerSeeding: Each player starts with a single cell in their corner of the board — every piece placed after that must touch territory you've already claimed.
common.scoring: The player who has claimed the most cells by the end of the game wins.
common.autoWinToggle: Auto-win: the game ends early once the outcome can no longer change, no matter what happens next.
common.smartAssistToggle: The “Smart …” option makes the random roll more likely to produce a move you can actually make once free space runs low late in the game — this keeps the game from stalling out on bad luck.
common.rotationToggle: Allow rotating pieces: when enabled, you can rotate a piece before placing it. Turn it off for a harder game — the piece can only be placed in the orientation it was drawn in.
common.eliminationNote: With three or four players, if someone runs out of possible moves, they're eliminated from the turn order (marked “eliminated”) — but their claimed cells and score stay on the board until the game ends.

dice.title: Diceritory — Rules
dice.objective: Claim territory on a rectangular board by placing rectangles whose dimensions are determined by a roll of the dice.
dice.turn: On your turn, roll two dice — they determine the width and height of the rectangle you must place this turn.
dice.placement: You can only place the rectangle so that at least one of its edges touches a cell you already control (vertically or horizontally).

toggles.title: Doubles grant an extra turn
toggles.body: If both dice come up the same, the same player immediately rolls again.

tetromino.title: Tetritory — Rules
tetromino.objective: The same battle for territory, but instead of rectangles you place the seven classic Tetris pieces.
tetromino.turn: On your turn, draw a piece from a 7-piece bag (each piece appears exactly once before the bag is reshuffled) and place it on the board.
tetromino.placement: You can only place the piece so that at least one of its cells touches a cell you already control (vertically or horizontally).

toggles.title: Auto-claim enclosed territory
toggles.body: If your pieces enclose an area of the board that your opponents can no longer reach, it's immediately filled as much as possible with tetrominoes in your color, and those cells are added to your score.

domino.title: Dominotory — Rules
domino.objective: The same battle for territory, but with dominoes made of two halves, each bearing its own number.
domino.turn: On your turn, roll a domino and place both of its halves on the board.
domino.placement: Besides the usual rule of touching your own territory, at least one half of the domino must touch an already-placed half bearing the same number — the numbers have to “match,” just like in classic dominoes.
domino.exampleCaption: Example: a new 4|5 domino is placed next to your own 2|4 domino so the two 4s touch.

toggles.title: Storage
toggles.body: You can set aside up to 4 dominoes instead of placing them right away, and retrieve any of them on any later turn of yours.

hex.title: Hexoritory — Rules
hex.objective: The same battle for territory, but on a board of hexagons, with shapes made up of them.
hex.turn: On your turn, the first die determines the shape (1 hex, 2 hexes, 3 in a line, or 3 in a triangle), and the second determines how many copies of that shape you must place this turn (1–3). All the shapes from the roll must be placed this same turn.
hex.placement: Each shape must touch your already-claimed territory along at least one hex edge (adjacency counts in all 6 directions).

toggles.title: Storage
toggles.body: You can stash one shape from the roll instead of placing it, if the storage slot is free. You can retrieve it on any later turn of yours — but you still have to place the other shapes from this roll.

toggles.title: Auto-claim enclosed territory
toggles.body: If your hexes enclose an area of the board that your opponents can no longer reach, it's immediately filled as much as possible with shapes in your color.

route.title: Routeritory — Rules
route.objective: The same battle for territory, but a river runs through the board, splitting it into three islands — two large ones where players start, and one small neutral one. Mountains are scattered here and there.
route.turn: On your turn, roll a die (1–6) — that's how many separate cells you must claim this turn, one at a time.
route.placement: A cell can only be claimed if it directly touches your already-claimed territory (including cells claimed earlier this same turn). The river and mountains are impassable. There are 3 bridges across the river (one per arm) — if your territory touches a bridge, you can immediately claim the free cell on the opposite bank.

toggles.title: Bridges
toggles.body: Every few rounds, all 3 bridges randomly relocate within their own arm of the river, so the crossing point doesn't stay put. A player with no possible move is eliminated, but if a bridge relocation opens up a crossing for them, they're immediately back in the game.

toggles.title: Matching-roll streak
toggles.body: If the die shows the same number three times in a row, that player gets an extra turn. But if the fourth roll in a row also matches, no further bonus is granted, and the streak starts counting fresh.

toggles.title: Auto-claim enclosed territory
toggles.body: If enabled: an area fully surrounded by a single player's own cells (river and mountains don't count as a border — only that player's own cells do) is immediately claimed for them, cell by cell.

hotseat.title: Hot-seat
hotseat.description: Local turn-based play — all players share one device.

bots.title: Vs. bots
bots.description: Play against computer opponents, choosing their number and difficulty.

online.title: Online
online.description: Create a lobby or join friends with a code.

choice.subtitle: Create a new lobby or join one with a code
choice.createHint: Set up the game and invite others with a code
choice.joinHint: Enter the code you were sent

join.title: Join lobby
join.subtitle: Enter the lobby code you were sent

errors.notFound: Lobby not found. Check the code and try again.
errors.alreadyStarted: The game in this lobby has already started.

lobby.minPlayersHint: At least 2 players are needed to start
lobby.waitingHint: Waiting for the lobby host to start the game…
